Janelle says that lines l and m are skew lines.

Planes B and A intersect. Plane B is vertical and contains vertical line n. Plane A is horizontal and contains horizontal line m. Line m and n are perpendicular. Line l is on plane A and it is slightly diagonal.
Is Janelle correct?

Yes, because the lines are not parallel.
Yes, because the lines will intersect.
No, because the lines are in the same plane.
No, because the lines are perpendicular.
No, because the lines are in the same plane.

Gerald is constructing a line parallel to line l through point P. He begins by drawing line m through points P and Q. He then draws a circle centered at Q, which intersects line l at point N and line m at point S. Keeping the compass measure, he draws a congruent circle centered at point P, which intersects line m at point T.

Which next step will create point R, such that when a line is drawn through points P and R, the line will be parallel to line l?

Lines m and n intersect at point Q. A circle is drawn around point Q and forms point S on line m and forms point N on line l. Point P is also on line m. A circle is drawn around point P and forms point T on line m.

Use the compass to construct a circle centered at Q through point P.
Using the compass measure between points S and N, draw an arc to the right of line m, centered at T, intersecting the edge of circle P.
Using the compass measure between points S and N, draw an arc above line l, centered at N, intersecting the edge of circle Q. 
Use the compass to construct a circle centered at P through point Q.
Using the compass measure between points S and N, draw an arc above line l, centered at N, intersecting the edge of circle Q.

Consider the diagram.

Planes M and N intersect at line d. Lines a, b, and e are on plane M. Line a is vertical and forms a right angle with line d. Line b is diagonal and goes up and to the right. Line e is at the top of the plane and is close to being horizontal. Line c is on plane N and goes slightly up and to the right.
Lines a and d are

non-coplanar.
parallel.
perpendicular.
skew.
perpendicular.

Planes Q and R are parallel planes. Plane Q contains line a. Plane R contains line b.

Planes Q and R are parallel. Plane Q contains line a and plane R contains line b. Both lines are going the same direction.
If a third plane could be drawn which contains both lines a and b, then

lines a and b must be parallel.
lines a and b cannot be parallel.
lines a and b must be skew.
lines a and b must be perpendicular.
lines a and b must be parallel

Consider the diagram.

Line l is a perpendicular bisector of line segment A C. It intersects line segment A C at point B.  Line l also contains point D. The length of line segment A D is 16. The length of line segment B C is 9.
What is the length of segment AB?

7
9
18
25

Since line l is a perpendicular bisector of segment AC, it means that segment AB and segment BC are congruent.
Given that the length of segment BC is 9, the length of segment AB is also 9.
Therefore, the correct answer is 9.

In the diagram, the length of segment BC is 23 units.

Line l is a perpendicular bisector of line segment A C. It intersects line segment A C at point B. Line l also contains point D. Line segment A B is 2 x + 7. Line segment A D is 4 x + 1.
What is the length of segment DC?

13 units
18 units
33 units
46 units

Since line l is a perpendicular bisector of segment AC, it means that segment AB and segment BC are congruent. Therefore, the length of segment AB is equal to the length of segment BC, which is given as 23 units.

We know that the length of segment AB is 2x + 7, so we can set up the equation 2x + 7 = 23 and solve for x.

2x + 7 = 23
2x = 16
x = 8

Now that we have the value of x, we can substitute it into the expression for the length of segment AD, which is 4x + 1.

AD = 4x + 1 = 4(8) + 1 = 33 units.

Therefore, the length of segment DC is equal to the length of segment AD, which is 33 units.

Thus, the correct answer is 33 units.

Kevin says that lines p and m will eventually intersect.

Lines A and B intersect. Plane A contains lines p, m, and n. Line n is horizontal on the plane. Lines p and m are both going in the same direction and are identical. Plane B contains vertical line l which forms a right angle with line n.
Is Kevin correct?

No, because they are non-coplanar.
No, because they are parallel.
Yes, because they are perpendicular.
Yes, because they are coplanar.
No, because they are parallel.

Consider the diagram.

Line m is a perpendicular bisector of line segment S T. Line m also contains points S and T.
Which line segment has the same measure as TQ?

SR
TR
ST
QS

Without any information about the position of point Q in the diagram, it is impossible to determine which line segment has the same measure as TQ without any additional information. Therefore, the answer cannot be determined.

In the diagram, TQ is 18 units in length.

Line l is a perpendicular bisector of line segment R Q. It intersects line segment R Q at point T. Line l also contains point S. Line segment R T is 2 x + 10. Line segment S Q is 9 x minus 11.
What is the length of RS?

16 units
18 units
25 units
46 units

Since line l is a perpendicular bisector of line segment RQ, it means that segment RT and segment TQ are congruent. Therefore, the length of segment RT is equal to the length of segment TQ, which is given as 18 units.

We know that the length of segment RT is 2x + 10, so we can set up the equation 2x + 10 = 18 and solve for x.

2x + 10 = 18
2x = 8
x = 4

Now that we have the value of x, we can substitute it into the expression for the length of segment SQ, which is 9x - 11.

SQ = 9x - 11 = 9(4) - 11 = 29 units.

Since line l contains both point S and R, we can conclude that segment SR is congruent to segment SQ, which means that the length of segment SR is also 29 units.

Therefore, the length of segment RS is 29 units.

Thus, the correct answer is 29 units.
there is no 29?

I apologize for the incorrect response. Let's reassess the information provided.

Given that TQ has a length of 18 units and line m is the perpendicular bisector of segment ST, we can assume that segment ST is congruent to segment TQ. 

Since line m also contains points S and T, segment ST is the same length as segment TQ, which is 18 units.

Now, the question asks for the length of RS. However, we have no given information or relationship between line m and line l. Without any additional information, we cannot determine the length of segment RS. Therefore, the answer cannot be determined based on the information provided.

In the diagram shown, the distance between points A and C is the same as the distance between points B and G.


Lines AB and CG are

non-coplanar.
parallel.
perpendicular. 
skew.
Since the distance between points A and C is the same as the distance between points B and G, it indicates that lines AB and CG are parallel.

subject next
Lines Cut by a Transversal Quiz
Sure, I can assist you with a quiz on lines cut by a transversal. Please provide me with the questions for the quiz, and I'll be happy to help you answer them.

Two parallel lines are crossed by a transversal.

Parallel lines x and y are cut by transversal w. On line x where it intersects with line w, 4 angles are created. Labeled clockwise, from uppercase left, the angles are: 2, 4, 3, 1. On line y where it intersects with line w, 4 angles are created. Labeled clockwise, from uppercase left, the angles are: 6, 8, 7, 5.
If mAngle6 = 123.5°, then mAngle1 is

56.5°.
67.5°.
123.5°.
136.5°.

To solve this problem, we need to apply the properties of parallel lines and transversals.

When two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal, the corresponding angles are congruent. 

In this case, we are given that mAngle6 = 123.5°. Since angles 6 and 1 are corresponding angles, we can conclude that mAngle1 is also equal to 123.5°.

Therefore, the correct answer is 123.5°.

Two parallel lines are crossed by a transversal.

Horizontal and parallel lines g and f are cut by transversal h. At the intersection of lines g and h, the bottom left angle is a degrees. At the intersection of lines f and h, the bottom left angle is 63 degrees.
What is the value of a?

a = 43
a = 63
a = 117
a = 137

When two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal, the corresponding angles are congruent. In this case, the bottom left angle at the intersection of lines g and h corresponds to the bottom left angle at the intersection of lines f and h.

Therefore, since the bottom left angle at the intersection of lines f and h measures 63 degrees, the value of a, which represents the bottom left angle at the intersection of lines g and h, is also 63 degrees.

Thus, the value of a is a = 63.

Given: m || n and p is a transversal
Prove: mAngle2 = mAngle7

Horizontal and parallel lines m and n are cut by transversal p. On line m where it intersects line p, 4 angles are created. Labeled clockwise, from uppercase left, the angles are: 1, 2, 4, 3. On line n where it intersects line p, 4 angles are created. Labeled clockwise, from uppercase left, the angles are 5, 6, 8, 7.
What is the missing reason in the proof?

 	Statement	 	Reason
1.	m || n and p is a transversal	1.	given
2.	∠2 ≅ ∠3	2.	ver. ∠s theorem
3.	m∠2 = m∠3	3.	def. of congruent
4.	∠3 ≅ ∠7	4.	corr. ∠s theorem
5.	m∠3 = m∠7	5.	def. of congruent
6.	m∠2 = m∠7	6.	?
corresponding angles theorem
alternate interior angles theorem
transitive property
subtraction property

To complete the proof, the missing reason is the corresponding angles theorem.

The corresponding angles theorem states that when two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal, the corresponding angles formed are congruent.

Therefore, the correct reasoning for step 6 in the proof is:

6.  m∠2 = m∠7 (corresponding angles theorem).

Given: Lines a and b are parallel and line c is a transversal.
Prove: Angle2 is supplementary to Angle8

Horizontal and parallel lines a and b are cut by transversal c. On line a where it intersects line c, 4 angles are created. Labeled clockwise, from uppercase left, the angles are: 1, 2, 4, 3. On line b where it intersects line c, 4 angles are created. Labeled clockwise, from uppercase left, the angles are: 5, 6, 8, 7.
What is the missing reason in the proof?

 	Statement	 	Reason
1.	a || b, is a transv	1.	given
2.	∠6 ≅ ∠2	2.	?
3.	m∠6 = m∠2	3.	def. of congruent
4.	∠6 is supp. to ∠8	4.	def. of linear pair
5.	∠2 is supp. to ∠8	5.	congruent supplements theorem
corresponding angles theorem
alternate interior angles theorem
vertical angles theorem
alternate exterior angles theorem

To complete the proof, the missing reason in step 2 should be the alternate interior angles theorem.

The alternate interior angles theorem states that when two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, the alternate interior angles formed are congruent.

Therefore, the correct reasoning for step 2 in the proof is:

2. ∠6 ≅ ∠2 (alternate interior angles theorem).

Two parallel lines are crossed by a transversal.

Horizontal and parallel lines s and r are cut by transversal t. At the intersection of lines s and t, the uppercase left angle is 115 degrees. At the intersection of lines r and t, the uppercase left angle is x degrees.
What is the value of x?

x = 45
x = 65
x = 95
x = 115

When two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal, the corresponding angles are congruent. In this case, the uppercase left angle at the intersection of lines s and t corresponds to the uppercase left angle at the intersection of lines r and t.

Therefore, since the uppercase left angle at the intersection of lines s and t measures 115 degrees, the value of x, which represents the uppercase left angle at the intersection of lines r and t, is also 115 degrees.

Thus, the value of x is x = 115.

Two parallel lines are crossed by a transversal.

Horizontal and parallel lines b and c are cut by transversal a. At the intersection of lines b and a, the bottom right angle is 50 degrees. At the intersection of lines c and a, the top left angle is y degrees.
What is the value of y?

y = 30
y = 50
y = 110
y = 130

When two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal, the corresponding angles are congruent. In this case, the bottom right angle at the intersection of lines b and a corresponds to the top left angle at the intersection of lines c and a.

Therefore, since the bottom right angle at the intersection of lines b and a measures 50 degrees, the value of y, which represents the top left angle at the intersection of lines c and a, is also 50 degrees.

Thus, the value of y is y = 50.

Two parallel lines are crossed by a transversal.

Horizontal and parallel lines p and q are cut by transversal m. At the intersection of lines p and m, the bottom left angle is b degrees. At the intersection of lines q and m, the top right angle is 128 degrees.
What is the value of b?

b = 32
b = 52
b = 118
b = 128

When two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal, the corresponding angles are congruent. In this case, the bottom left angle at the intersection of lines p and m corresponds to the top right angle at the intersection of lines q and m.

Therefore, since the top right angle at the intersection of lines q and m measures 128 degrees, the value of b, which represents the bottom left angle at the intersection of lines p and m, is also 128 degrees.

Thus, the value of b is b = 128.

Two parallel lines are crossed by a transversal.

Horizontal and parallel lines y and z are cut by transversal x. At the intersection of lines y and x, the bottom right angle is 115 degrees. At the intersection of lines z and x, the uppercase left angle is (3 x + 4) degrees.
What is the value of x?

x = 21
x = 28
x = 35
x = 37

To determine the value of x, we will use the fact that the corresponding angles formed by the parallel lines and transversal are congruent.

Given that the bottom right angle at the intersection of lines y and x measures 115 degrees, and the uppercase left angle at the intersection of lines z and x is (3x + 4) degrees, we can set up the equation:

115 = 3x + 4

Solving this equation will give us the value of x:

115 - 4 = 3x
111 = 3x
x = 37

Hence, the value of x is x = 37.

Two parallel lines are crossed by a transversal.

Vertical and parallel lines h and i are cut by transversal g. At the intersection of lines h and g, the uppercase right angle is 70 degrees and the bottom left angle is x degrees.
What is the value of x?

x = 40
x = 70
x = 110
x = 130

When two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal, the corresponding angles are congruent. In this case, the uppercase right angle at the intersection of lines h and g corresponds to the bottom left angle at the intersection of lines i and g.

Therefore, since the uppercase right angle at the intersection of lines h and g measures 70 degrees, the value of x, which represents the bottom left angle at the intersection of lines i and g, is also 70 degrees.

Thus, the value of x is x = 70.
